projectName: Git Branching Strategy, Versioning Scheme & CI/CD Pipeline
description: Establish a structured Git branching workflow with three permanent branches (stable, testing, developing), a custom versioning scheme, and GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for the Neuron Chrome Extension.

userStories.title: Version bump script
userStories.description: Create a script that handles version bumping so that the CI and local workflows can reliably update version strings.
acceptanceCriteria: A script scripts/bump-version.sh accepts parameters: major/minor increment, branch suffix, commit hash
Script updates manifest.json version (X.Y.Z), manifest.json version_name (custom format X.XX.XXXXy), and VERSION file
Script can be run locally for manual version bumps
Script is idempotent (running twice with same params produces same result)
Script uses jq to safely modify manifest.json

userStories.title: Implement custom versioning scheme
userStories.description: Implement version format X.XX.XXXXy (X = major, XX = minor features, XXXX = first 4 chars of commit hash, y = s/b/d for branch) in manifest.json and VERSION file.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json version field uses Chrome strict X.Y.Z format (e.g., 2.1.0)
manifest.json version_name field uses custom format (e.g., 2.01.a3f4s)
A root VERSION file contains the custom version string
The suffix is s for stable, b for testing, d for developing
The 4-char commit hash portion updates automatically via bump-version.sh

userStories.title: Display version in popup UI
userStories.description: Display the current extension version in the popup so users know which build they are running.
acceptanceCriteria: The popup page displays the custom version_name from manifest.json
Version label is visible without scrolling (e.g., footer or header of popup)
Version updates correctly after each new build

userStories.title: CI pipeline - Validate on push/PR
userStories.description: Run validation (lint, manifest checks) on every push and PR so that broken code is caught early.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on push to developing, testing, stable
GitHub Actions workflow triggers on PRs targeting any of the three branches
Workflow validates manifest.json is well-formed
Workflow runs any configured linters
Failed checks block PR merges

userStories.title: CI pipeline - Auto-update version on merge
userStories.description: Automatically update version string when code is merged into any of the three main branches.
acceptanceCriteria: On merge to developing, version is stamped with suffix d and current commit hash
On merge to testing, version is stamped with suffix b and current commit hash
On merge to stable, version is stamped with suffix s and current commit hash
Both manifest.json (version_name) and VERSION file are updated
The Chrome version field (X.Y.Z) is incremented appropriately (patch bump on stable merges)

userStories.title: CI pipeline - Auto-promote developing to testing
userStories.description: Automatically promote code from developing to testing when all CI checks pass.
acceptanceCriteria: When all checks pass on a push/merge to developing, a PR is auto-created from developing to testing
The auto-PR includes a summary of commits being promoted
If an open promotion PR already exists, it is updated rather than duplicated

userStories.title: CI pipeline - Build release artifact for stable
userStories.description: Build a .zip artifact automatically when code is merged to stable.
acceptanceCriteria: On merge to stable, workflow packages the extension into a .zip file
The .zip excludes dev-only files (e.g., .github/, tasks/, tests/, .claude/)
The .zip filename includes the version string (e.g., neuron-2.01.a3f4s.zip)
The artifact is uploaded to the GitHub Actions run

userStories.title: CI pipeline - Create GitHub Release with changelog
userStories.description: Create a GitHub Release automatically on stable merges with an auto-generated changelog.
acceptanceCriteria: A GitHub Release is created with the version tag (e.g., v2.01.a3f4s)
The release includes the .zip artifact as a downloadable asset
The release body contains an auto-generated changelog (commits since last stable release)
The release is marked as latest

userStories.title: Hotfix workflow
userStories.description: Support hotfix branches off stable that merge back to both stable and developing.
acceptanceCriteria: Hotfix branches are named hotfix/<description> and branch from stable
On merge of a hotfix to stable, CI auto-creates a PR to merge the same fix into developing
Version is bumped as a patch release on stable
The hotfix triggers the same release artifact and GitHub Release creation as a normal stable merge

userStories.title: Create permanent branch structure
userStories.description: Create three permanent branches (stable, testing, developing) so that code flows through a structured promotion pipeline. NOTE: This story requires GitHub admin access and should be done manually or after all CI workflows are in place.
acceptanceCriteria: Branch developing exists and is the default working branch for new features
Branch testing exists as the integration/QA stage
Branch stable exists as the production-ready branch
Repository default branch is set to developing
Branch protection rules prevent direct pushes to stable and testing
